county_metadata.parent_county: St. Louis County (separated as independent city)
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri independent city formed 1876 from St. Louis County (separated as independent city). County seat: St. Louis. French colonial heritage — area settled under French and Spanish governance before Louisiana Purchase (1803).
county_metadata.research_tips: St. Louis City is an independent city, separate from St. Louis County since 1876 — the only such arrangement in Missouri. The City of St. Louis Recorder of Deeds holds land records. St. Louis City Hall and the Missouri State Archives hold vital records. The Missouri Historical Society in Forest Park has extraordinary manuscript collections, family papers, and photographs. St. Louis Public Library's genealogy collection is extensive. St. Louis Genealogical Society is one of the most active in the nation. Old Courthouse (where Dred Scott case was tried) is now part of Gateway Arch National Park. Calvary and Bellefontaine cemeteries have exceptional burial records. French colonial records from 1764 founding. St. Louis Cathedral (Old Cathedral) parish records from the 1770s. Major immigration gateway — German, Irish, Italian, Czech, Polish, and African American communities all have extensive institutional records. City directories from 1821. Naturalization records at NARA-KC and Missouri State Archives. National Personnel Records Center (NPRC) in St. Louis holds military personnel records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Missouri State Archives microfilm and digitized records for St. Louis City from colonial era

source_profiles.source_name: St. Louis City Deed Records -- City of St. Louis Recorder of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: City of St. Louis Recorder of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1876 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records intact from county formation (1876)

source_profiles.source_name: St. Louis City Probate Records -- St. Louis City Probate Court
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, administrations, and guardianship records from 1876. Missouri probate courts also handle some adoption records.

source_profiles.source_name: St. Louis City Marriage Records -- Recorder of Deeds / FamilySearch
source_profiles.repository: City of St. Louis Recorder of Deeds / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and records from 1876. Missouri marriage records kept at county level by Recorder of Deeds. FamilySearch has indexed many Missouri marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: St. Louis City Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1876-1950 for St. Louis City. Missouri territorial census 1820; state census 1844, 1852, 1856, 1860, 1864, 1868, 1876 (incomplete). Slave schedules 1850, 1860.

source_profiles.source_name: St. Louis City Enslaved Persons and Freedom Suit Records
source_profiles.repository: Missouri State Archives / Washington University Digital Gateway
source_profiles.coverage_notes: St. Louis was a center of slavery and freedom litigation. Over 300 freedom suits filed in St. Louis Circuit Court, including the Dred Scott case. Estate inventories, slave schedules, bills of sale, manumission records, and African American church records. Records predate the 1876 city-county separation.

source_profiles.source_name: Native American Records -- St. Louis City Area
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-removal records for multiple tribes (Osage, Missouri, Shawnee, Delaware, Sauk, Fox) in the St. Louis City area. Includes Indian agency records, treaty records, removal records, and trade ledgers. Platte Purchase (1836) records at NARA-KC.

source_profiles.source_name: Civil War Records -- St. Louis City
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Confederate and Union service records, loyalty oaths, provost marshal records, pension applications, and guerrilla warfare documentation for St. Louis City area. Missouri's border state status means both Union and Confederate records exist.

source_profiles.source_name: French and Spanish Colonial Court Records -- St. Louis City Area
source_profiles.repository: Missouri State Archives, Jefferson City / Ste. Genevieve Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: French colonial court records, Spanish colonial land grants, parish registers, and administrative records for the St. Louis City area. Louisiana Purchase (1803) transferred governance to the US.

source_profiles.source_name: German Church Records -- St. Louis City
source_profiles.repository: Local congregations / Concordia Historical Institute / Missouri State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German Evangelical, Lutheran, Catholic, and Reformed church records for St. Louis City. Baptisms, marriages, burials, and confirmations. Many records in German language. Concordia Historical Institute (Lutheran Church-Missouri Synod) in St. Louis holds synod records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1812 from Original district. County seat: Clayton. French colonial heritage — area settled under French and Spanish governance before Louisiana Purchase (1803).
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Clayton holds land records from 1812 (original district). St. Louis County Library headquarters in Kirkwood has one of the premier genealogical collections in the nation — the St. Louis County Library Special Collections holds microfilm, indexes, city directories, family files, and statewide resources. St. Louis County separated from St. Louis City in 1876. Missouri Historical Society in Forest Park (St. Louis) holds extensive manuscript collections. The county has been heavily suburban since the mid-20th century but earlier records reflect farming and small-town communities. French colonial land grants, German immigrant records, and diverse ethnic community records are all important. FamilySearch microfilm is extensive.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Missouri State Archives microfilm and digitized records for St. Louis County from colonial era

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1812 from Original district. County seat: Ste. Genevieve. French colonial heritage — area settled under French and Spanish governance before Louisiana Purchase (1803).
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Ste. Genevieve holds land records from 1812 (original district). Ste. Genevieve is the oldest permanent European settlement in Missouri, founded by French colonists circa 1735. The town is now a National Historical Park. French colonial records are exceptional — baptisms, marriages, burials at Ste. Genevieve Catholic Church from the 1750s, and French colonial court records at Missouri State Archives. Spanish colonial land grants (1762-1800) at Missouri State Archives. Ste. Genevieve Archives and Museum have outstanding local records. Lead mining records from Mine La Motte and other early mines. The Centre for French Colonial Studies has published colonial-era records. FamilySearch microfilm is extensive. French-language records require translation skills.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Missouri State Archives microfilm and digitized records for Ste. Genevieve from colonial era

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1835 from Cape Girardeau. County seat: Bloomfield. Missouri Bootheel (southeastern lowlands) — cotton agriculture region.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Bloomfield holds land records from 1835. Stoddard County is in southeast Missouri between the Ozark foothills and the Bootheel lowlands. Stoddard County Historical Society in Bloomfield has genealogical files. Crowley's Ridge runs through the county. Check Cape Girardeau County for pre-1835 records. Strong Southern settlement from Tennessee and Kentucky. Timber and cotton agriculture. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1851 from Taney. County seat: Galena. Ozark Highlands region with rugged terrain and Southern Appalachian settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Galena holds land records from 1851. Stone County is adjacent to Branson (Taney County) in the Ozarks. Table Rock Lake (1958) flooded former communities — check Corps of Engineers relocation records. Stone County Historical Society has genealogical files. Check Taney County for pre-1851 records. Strong Southern Appalachian settlement heritage. White River valley settlement. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records.

county_metadata.record_losses: Civil War guerrilla destruction; courthouse burned 1865; most early records lost
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1837 from Greene. County seat: Forsyth. Ozark Highlands region with rugged terrain and Southern Appalachian settlement. Burned county: Civil War guerrilla destruction; courthouse burned 1865; most early records lost
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Forsyth holds records restarting after the 1865 courthouse burning during Civil War guerrilla destruction. For pre-1865 records, check Greene County (parent), Missouri State Archives, and BLM GLO land patents. Taney County is now famous for Branson tourism but was historically a remote Ozark county. White River Valley Historical Society in Branson has genealogical files. Baldknobbers (vigilante group, 1880s) operated here. Bull Shoals and Table Rock Lakes flooded earlier settlements. Strong Southern Appalachian settlement heritage. FamilySearch microfilm covers post-war records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1837

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1845 from Shannon, Wright. County seat: Houston. Ozark Highlands region with rugged terrain and Southern Appalachian settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Houston holds records restarting after Civil War courthouse destruction. For pre-war records, check Shannon and Wright counties (parents), Missouri State Archives, and BLM GLO land patents. Texas County is the largest county in Missouri by area. Deep Ozark terrain with sparse settlement. Texas County Historical Society in Houston has post-war genealogical files. Strong Southern Appalachian settlement heritage. Current River headwaters area. Montauk Spring and Jack's Fork River. FamilySearch microfilm covers post-war records. Church records are essential supplements.

county_metadata.record_losses: Order No. 11 (1863) devastation; courthouse burned; most pre-war records destroyed
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1855 from Bates. County seat: Nevada. Affected by Order No. 11 (1863) — forced depopulation of western Missouri border counties during Civil War. Burned county: Order No. 11 (1863) devastation; courthouse burned; most pre-war records destroyed
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Nevada holds records restarting after Order No. 11 (1863) devastation. Vernon County was one of the most severely affected counties along the Kansas-Missouri border. The courthouse was burned and most pre-war records destroyed. For pre-1863 records, check Bates County (parent), Missouri State Archives, and BLM GLO records. Bushwhacker Museum in Nevada documents the border war. Vernon County Historical Society has post-war genealogical files. The county was named for Miles Vernon, not Mount Vernon. FamilySearch microfilm covers post-war records. Strong Southern settlement heritage.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1855

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1833 from Montgomery. County seat: Warrenton. Missouri German Belt — significant German immigrant settlement from the 1830s-1860s.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Warrenton holds land records from 1833. Warren County is in east-central Missouri along the Missouri River. Warren County Historical Society in Warrenton has genealogical files. Strong German immigrant heritage — Marthasville and other communities. Daniel Boone Historical Area nearby. Check Montgomery County for pre-1833 records. German Reformed and Lutheran church records are important. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records. Wine country heritage.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Warren County. Missouri is a public land state — BLM GLO records document first transfer of land from federal government. Some areas have earlier French/Spanish colonial grants.

source_profiles.source_name: German Church Records -- Warren County
source_profiles.repository: Local congregations / Concordia Historical Institute / Missouri State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German Evangelical, Lutheran, Catholic, and Reformed church records for Warren. Baptisms, marriages, burials, and confirmations. Many records in German language. Concordia Historical Institute (Lutheran Church-Missouri Synod) in St. Louis holds synod records.

source_profiles.source_name: Warren County Tax Records -- Missouri State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists and personal property assessments from 1833. List heads of household, property, livestock, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Useful as census substitutes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Generally well preserved at Missouri State Archives
